strengths: Demonstrated hands-on experience building agentic workflows and AI-infrastructure tools at re:cinq, including MCP code generators.
Strong backend and distributed systems background with real-time, high-throughput systems from Optilyz and Dunnhumby, including event-driven architectures.
Proven track record in open-source technical leadership and engineering culture building through GISCollective, workshops, and community contributions.
concerns: Current career gap since October 2025 raises questions about recent active engagement in production systems.
Lack of explicit evidence of leading engineering teams beyond 10–15 people or managing outsourced engineering governance at scale.
No clear demonstration of direct experience in regulated fintech environments, credit risk systems, or financial compliance infrastructure.
response.justification: The candidate brings strong technical depth in distributed systems and real-world agentic AI tooling, with a culture of engineering excellence and open-source leadership, but lacks demonstrable experience in regulated fintech environments and scaling technical leadership to the executive level required for this role.
